title: File is not writable on network share
symptom: When attempting to create or modify a file on a network share, n8n reports 'The file is not writable'. This issue is specific to certain servers.
applies_when: Trying to write to a file on a specific network share
not_this_if: File writing works on other servers
root_cause: The error indicates that the n8n process does not have the necessary permissions to write to the specified file location. This can be due to incorrect user permissions or misconfigured access restrictions in n8n.
fix_steps: Check the user account under which n8n is running.
Ensure this account has write permissions to the network share.
Consider mapping the network share to a drive letter for easier access.
Test writing to the file again.
agent_instruction: Verify and adjust the permissions for the user running n8n on the network share.
